title: Invoca Tag on Vendor Landing Page Subdomain (ads.sinklaw.com)
description:
Domain: https://www.sinklaw.com

Context
The client’s marketing vendor is building landing pages on their platform and hosting them on the subdomain ads.sinklaw.com.

A previous task was completed to set up the subdomain connection using:
  • Host: ads
  • Target: sites.ludicrous.cloud
These landing pages will live on the vendor platform but appear under the branded subdomain.

Request

Now that the subdomain has been created, we want to deploy an Invoca tracking script via Google Tag Manager so we can use dynamic call tracking numbers on those landing pages.

Before submitting a ticket, I wanted to confirm feasibility since the pages are vendor-hosted.

Questions
  1. Since the pages are hosted on the vendor platform but under ads.sinklaw.com, will our existing GTM container for sinklaw.com load on those pages?
  2. If not, would the vendor need to:
    • install our GTM container, or
    • place the Invoca script directly in their page template?
  3. If GTM will work on the subdomain, we’d like to deploy the following Invoca script:
<script>
  (function(i,n,v,o,c,a) { i.InvocaTagId = o; var s = n.createElement('script'); s.type = 'text/javascript';
    s.async = true; s.src = ('https:' === n.location.protocol ? 'https://' : 'http://' ) + v;
    var fs = n.getElementsByTagName('script')[0]; fs.parentNode.insertBefore(s, fs);
  })(window, document, 'solutions.invocacdn.com/js/invoca-latest.min.js', '2377/0749246453');
</script>

Goal

Enable dynamic call tracking via Invoca on the vendor landing pages hosted on ads.sinklaw.com.

Let me know the best path forward, and I can submit the appropriate ticket if needed or redirect the client!

title: Verify CTV/OTT Tracking Scripts and Pixel Load Behavior
assignees: Justyna Acosta, Keith Bruss, Matthew Nyahari
description:
Relevant Tasks:
  • Add OTT Pixels via GTM - J400 George Sink Injury Lawyers
  • Tracking Pixel Inconsistency Review - J400 George Sink Injury Lawyers
Context:
Sink Law’s CTV/OTT vendor is reporting that tracking appears to be improved, but still lower than expected. They are asking Clixsy to confirm whether any tracking scripts or pixels are still being lazy-loaded or deferred, and specifically noted an Octane pixel “missing pid parameter” error.
Key Questions / Clarifications Needed: 
  • Can development confirm whether Google Tag Manager and all relevant tracking/pixel scripts are currently loading immediately on page load across the site?
  • What is the cause of the “missing pid parameter” error, and can we resolve it on Clixsy's end?
Relevant Client Message:
 
“We’ve been noticing a change in conversions (good), but not at the level we would expect... I noticed there are some console errors and we would like to confirm whether any scripts or pixels are still being lazy loaded or deferred. In particular, the Octane pixel is showing a ‘missing pid parameter,’ which can sometimes occur if tracking scripts are delayed or excluded from the global header. Can you please have your web team confirm again that all tracking pixels load immediately in the global header and are excluded from lazy loading or script deferral?”

title: GSC Alert: 5xx Server Errors Blocking Indexing
description:
Context: George Sink received a Google Search Console alert on March 11 notifying them that pages in their sitemap are being blocked from indexing due to Server error (5xx). The client's CMO, Trudy Emlaw, forwarded the alert to us. The issue has not yet been investigated internally and appears to still be active. 

Key Questions / Clarifications Needed: 
  • Are 5xx errors currently present on sinklaw.com, and if so, which pages are affected?
  • Is this a server/hosting issue, an SEO configuration issue, or both?
  • What is the scope of impact — how many pages are affected?
  • What is the recommended fix and who should own it (dev vs. SEO)?

Relevant Client Message: Google Search Console alert forwarded by Trudy Emlaw (CMO) — GSC identified new indexing prevention reason: Server error (5xx). Client was notified via GSC on March 11, 2026.

title: Yelp Phone Number Strategy + Directory Setup Best Practices
description:
Sink Law is in the process of setting up 17 Yelp location profiles. Gabby is also setting up listings across legal directories (Avvo, Martindale, Super Lawyers, Justia, etc.). 

Before moving forward, we want to confirm the correct phone number strategy and ensure all listings are aligned with SEO + tracking best practices.  

Background from Client 

Client question: 
When setting up profiles for each of our 17 locations on Yelp, should we:
  1. Use one call tracking number across all profiles
     OR
  2. Use the phone number listed on each corresponding GBP?
Are there any other best practices we should be aware of when setting these up?

Discussion Questions for Mikey
  1. For Yelp specifically:
    • Should each location use its corresponding GBP phone number?
    • Or should we use a tracking number?
    • If tracking is used, what is the correct structure to avoid NAP inconsistency?
  2. For legal directories (Avvo, Martindale, Justia, Super Lawyers, etc.):
    • Don't we set these up on behalf of the attorneys? If we do, please confirm, otherwise, please let me know about the following:
      • Should these mirror GBP exactly for NAP consistency?
      • Are there exceptions?
      • Any citation consistency risks to be aware of?
  3. Any known Yelp-specific risks (suspension triggers, filtering issues, duplicate suppression, etc.)?
  4. Any citation or local SEO best practices we should confirm before all 17 go live?
Goal

We want to ensure:
  • NAP consistency across all 17 locations
  • Proper call tracking implementation (if applicable)
  • No future citation cleanup issues
  • Clean first-time setup (avoid retroactive fixes)

Thank you !!

title: Recommended Approach for Preventing Duplicate Litify Intakes
description:
Background:
The client is reviewing webform submissions and noticed that some users appear to be creating multiple intakes in Litify from duplicate submissions. 

Keith confirmed in the email thread that there is currently nothing preventing duplicate submissions from triggering the Litify webhook. He suggested three potential approaches: 
  1. Front-end prevention: disable the submit button after the first click to prevent accidental double submissions.
  2. De-duplication logic: track submissions by email (or another identifier) and suppress repeat Litify webhooks.
  3. Litify-side controls: configure Litify to treat Email + Source as a unique identifier and merge/prevent duplicates.
The client responded that Option 1 seems like a good place to start and asked whether they should implement all three or if selecting one option is sufficient.
 
They are still reviewing the data and mentioned that some duplicate contacts appear to have been entered manually rather than through the API.
 
Question:
Before I confirm next steps with the client, do we recommend starting with front-end prevention only, or should we plan for any additional safeguards (such as de-duplication logic or Litify-side configuration)? I've submitted the ticket for front-end prevention, but wanted to follow up on the rest of Trudy's questions.

title: Tracking Pixel Inconsistency Review
description:
Seeking help check the client reported issue please.

Domain: georgesinklaw.com
Primary URL(s): Sitewide – all pages where tracking should fire

Summary:
The client’s ad team has observed several days of lower-than-expected pixel fires despite campaigns driving engagement. Pixels are currently installed and active; however, there may be a delay or inconsistency in how tracking scripts load on page load. They have suggested lazy loading may be the culprit.

They would like the web team to review the implementation to ensure tracking fires immediately and consistently across all visits.

Client Goal:
Ensure all tracking pixels fire reliably on page load so visits and conversions are fully captured without delay or missed interactions.

Requirements Checklist:
  • All tracking pixels load and fire consistently on page load
  • Tracking functions across desktop and mobile
  • Confirmation provided once fixes are verified

Thanks much!

title: New Breadcrumbs structured data issues detected for https://www.sinklaw.com/
description:

Summary:
Google Search Console detected a critical Breadcrumbs structured data issue on the Sink Law website. The error states: Missing field "item" (in "itemListElement"). This may prevent breadcrumb enhancements from appearing in search results.

Client Goal:
Ensure breadcrumb structured data is valid and compliant so pages are eligible for enhanced search result display and avoid any search visibility limitations. 

Requirements Checklist:
Review breadcrumb structured data across the site
Confirm whether issue is already resolved or requires correction
If correction is required, implement fix
Confirm resolution or status update for client communication

Breadcrumbs structured data issues detected in https://www.sinklaw.com/
To the owner of https://www.sinklaw.com/:
Search Console has identified that your site is affected by 1 Breadcrumbs structured data issue(s). The following issues were found on your site.

Top critical issues*

Missing field "item" (in "itemListElement")
*Critical issues prevent your page or feature from appearing in Search results.
We recommend that you fix these issues when possible to enable the best experience and coverage in Google Search.
Fix Breadcrumbs structured data issues


Thanks much!

title: Updating the Logo
description:
Hi!

We got a request from Trudy to update the logo on the George Sink website: https://www.sinklaw.com/

Here's the new logo:



Alternatively, there's a red version we could use, but Trudy and I agreed the white would look best.

Given previous feedback from the dev team, I know this site is new to us and not truly onboarded, but we'd like to do what we can to get some change requests processed in order to demonstrate value to the client and secure the contract at the rate Corey is pitching.

That said, I've been told site changes can be cumbersome due to the half-in nature of the site. Would updating the logo in the header and footer be possible as a change request task? If not, I at least want it on the team's radar so it's queued.

Let me know, and I can get this sent in as a task via email if it's doable. Thank you!

Form submissions from the rogue site are being forwarded to the client's team, confirming the site is live. The client has asked Clixsy to take over and push this forward.

Clixsy Goal: Remove georgiacarwreck.com from search results and resolve the brand impersonation issue causing consumer confusion.

title: Prevent Duplicate Webform Submissions
description:
Domain: sinklaw.com

The client is reviewing webform submissions and noticed that some users appear to be creating multiple intakes in Litify from duplicate submissions. They asked whether anything currently prevents multiple submissions from creating multiple Litify intakes and whether other factors could prevent a webform submission from generating an intake.


Keith responded in the thread confirming that there is currently nothing preventing duplicate submissions from triggering the Litify webhook. As a preventative measure, he recommended implementing front-end prevention by disabling the submit button after the first click to reduce accidental double submissions.


The client agreed that this would be a good place to start.


Client Goal:
Reduce duplicate Litify intakes caused by accidental multiple form submissions.


Requirements Checklist

  • Forms that generate Litify intakes prevent multiple submissions caused by repeated clicks during processing

  • Submit button becomes inactive after the initial submission attempt

  • User receives clear visual feedback indicating the form is processing

  • Valid form submissions continue to trigger the Litify webhook and create an intake as expected

title: Install  TikTok plugin
description:
The client plans to begin running TikTok ads and has requested that the TikTok plugin be installed on their website so a TikTok pixel can be used for ad tracking. They have sufficient WordPress access to configure the plugin themselves once it's installed.

 Domain:
sinklaw.com


Client Goal:

Enable TikTok ad tracking by having the TikTok plugin installed on the website so the client can create and manage their TikTok pixel.


Requirements for Completion:

  • TikTok plugin is installed on the website and available within the WordPress environment.

  • The plugin allows the client to configure and connect their TikTok pixel for ad tracking.

  • Plugin installation does not negatively impact site functionality or performance.

  • Confirm the plugin is accessible in WordPress so the client can complete pixel configuration on their end.

Client email request:


"Would it be possible to install the TikTok plugin into our site? We plan on running TikTok ads, so we need to install it and create a pixel for these ads to run."

Client's screenshots of installation steps:

title: Create Subdomain for Vendor Landing Pages

The vendor provided instructions to connect a subdomain via DNS. Instead of the suggested “fb” host, we will create ads.sinklaw.com to support their landing pages and future campaigns. Trudy has approved this subdomain name.

Requirements Checklist:
  • Subdomain ads.sinklaw.com is created for the domain
  • The subdomain resolves correctly and connects to the vendor platform
  • The configuration allows the vendor to host landing pages on the subdomain
  • The subdomain is verified as active and reachable once DNS propagates

Vendor Instructions (Reference):
Vendor requested a CNAME connection using the following target:

Host: ads
Target: sites.ludicrous.cloud

Once completed, the vendor will need confirmation of the subdomain URL so they can complete their platform connection.

title: Salesforce Integration
description:
This is the task you asked me to create for the salesforce integration to work with the AM and client. The code being used in the sinklaw-2024 theme sends data from cf7 to https://sinklaw.my.salesforce-sites.com/api/services/apexrest/litify_pm/api/v1/intake/create and we need to update/remove the old emails in it.


Original task: Juvo Chat Code for Website - J400 George Sink Injury Lawyers

The chatbot they have is also apex chat bot.
add_action('wpcf7_mail_sent', 'send_to_crm', 10, 1);
function send_to_crm()
{
    $submission = WPCF7_Submission::get_instance();
    $data = $submission->get_posted_data();
    $caseType = "General";

    $formIdsToExclude = array(6722, 2490, 1574, 6709, 1660, 3103, 1655); //Scholarship and Free Download Forms
    
    $wpcf7 = WPCF7_ContactForm::get_current();
    $unmodifiedData = $data;
    if (!in_array($wpcf7->id, $formIdsToExclude)) {
        $nameSplit = explode(" ", trim($data["your-name"]));
        if(count($nameSplit) > 1){
            $firstName = $nameSplit[0];
            $lastName = implode("", array_slice($nameSplit, 1));
        }
        else if($data["first-name"] && $data["last-name"]){
            $firstName = $data["first-name"];
            $lastName = $data["last-name"];
        }
        else{
            $firstName = "";
            $lastName = $data["your-name"]; //Last Name is required
        }

        $message = "";
        if($data["your-message"]){
            $message = $data["your-message"];
        }
        else if($data["date-of-incident"] || $data["can-you-explain"]
            || $data["what-injuries"] || $data["currently-represented"]){
            $message = "Date of incident: " . $data["date-of-incident"] .
                " Explanation of what happened: " . $data["can-you-explain"] .
                " Injuries Sustained: " . $data["what-injuries"] .
                " Currently Represented by an attorney: " . ($data["currently-represented"] ? $data["currently-represented"][0] : "");
        } else if($data["Didyouwork"] || $data["reside"] || $data["menu-908"] || $data["are-you-represented"]){
            $message = "Did you work or reside at camp LeJeune before 1988? " . ($data["Didyouwork"] ? $data["Didyouwork"][0] : "") .
                " How long did you work or reside at camp LeJeune?" . ($data["reside"] ? $data["reside"][0] : "") .
                " Injuries Sustained: " . ($data["menu-908"] ? $data["menu-908"][0] : "") .
                " Currently Represented by an attorney: " . ($data["are-you-represented"] ? $data["are-you-represented"][0] : "");
        }

        // sms opt in
        $sms_output = false;
        if ( $data["textMessageOptIn"] ) {
            $sms = $data["textMessageOptIn"];
            if ( $sms[0] === 'Yes, I consent to receiving SMS messages' ) {
                $sms_output = true;
            }
        }

        $data = [
            "firstName" => $firstName,
            "lastName" => $lastName,
            "email" => $data["your-email"],
            "phone" => $data["your-phone"],
            "description" => $message,
            "Comments" => "",
            "intakeStatus" => "New",
            "CaseTypeMain" => $caseType,
            "CaseSubType" => "",
            "client_id__c" => $data["customer-id"],
            "textMessageOptIn" => $sms_output,
            "APISource" => "Website",
            "API_Source" => "Website",
            "API_Source__c" => "Website",
            "Referring_URL" => $data["Referring_URL"],
            "Referring_URL__c" => $data["Referring_URL"],
            "API" => true
        ];
        $url = "https://sinklaw.my.salesforce-sites.com/api/services/apexrest/litify_pm/api/v1/intake/create";
        $ch = curl_init($url);
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_HTTPHEADER, array('Content-Type: application/json'));
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS, json_encode($data));
        curl_setopt($ch, CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER, true);
        $result = curl_exec($ch);
        $intakeID = 'NA'; //This is for Camp Lejune
        $responseCode = curl_getinfo($ch, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);
        error_log("Litify integration result. Response code: " . $responseCode . " Result:" . var_export($result, true) . " Data:" . var_export(json_encode($data), true));
        if ($responseCode != 200) {
            wp_mail("taylor.martin@brainbytescreative.com,franky@brainbytescreative.com, JJacoby@sinklaw.com", "Litify integration failure", "Result:" . var_export($result, true) . " Data:" . var_export($data, true));
        }else{
            $intakeID = json_decode($result)->intakeId;
            //wp_mail("taylor.martin@brainbytescreative.com", "Litify integration success", "Result:" . var_export($result, true) . " Data:" . var_export($data, true));
        }
        /*
        if($wpcf7->id == 17720){
            $campCurl = curl_init();
            $token = camp_lejeune_get_access_token();
            if (!empty($_SERVER['HTTP_CLIENT_IP'])) {
                $ip = $_SERVER['HTTP_CLIENT_IP'];
            } elseif (!empty($_SERVER['HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR'])) {
                $ip = $_SERVER['HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR'];
            } else {
                $ip = $_SERVER['REMOTE_ADDR'];
            }
            if(!$token){
                wp_mail("engineering.oncall@hennessey.com,egzon@hennessey.com,alex@hennessey.com", "Camp Lejeune integration failed on Sinklaw.com due to the access token", var_export($token , true));
            }

            $jsonData = [
                "campaign_id" => "1125",
                "full_name" =>  $unmodifiedData['first-name'] . ' ' . $unmodifiedData['last-name'],
                "phone" => $unmodifiedData['your-phone'],
                "email" => $unmodifiedData['your-email'],
                "date" => date("Y-m-d H:i:s"),
                "questions"=> [ 
                    "consent" => "1",
                    "user_ip" => $ip,
                    "Were you or a loved one exposed to contaminated drinking water at Camp Lejeune or MCAS New River for 30 or more days between August 1, 1953 – December 31, 1987?" => $unmodifiedData['Didyouwork'][0],
                    "How long were you at Camp Lejeune between 1953 and 1987?" => $unmodifiedData['reside'][0],
                    "Do you suffer from any of the following conditions?" => $unmodifiedData['menu-908'][0],
                    "Do you suffer from any other conditions? If so, please list the condition(s)" => $unmodifiedData['other-conditions'],
                    "Are you currently being represented by another firm with regards to this case?" => $unmodifiedData['are-you-represented'][0],
                    "Partner_Id" => $intakeID
                ]
            ];

            $jsonData = json_encode($jsonData , true);

            curl_setopt_array($campCurl, array(
                CURLOPT_URL => 'https://pcdialer.camginc.com/api/leads/',
                CURLOPT_RETURNTRANSFER => true,
                CURLOPT_ENCODING => '',
                CURLOPT_MAXREDIRS => 10,
                CURLOPT_TIMEOUT => 0,
                CURLOPT_FOLLOWLOCATION => true,
                CURLOPT_HTTP_VERSION => CURL_HTTP_VERSION_1_1,
                CURLOPT_CUSTOMREQUEST => 'POST',
                CURLOPT_POSTFIELDS => $jsonData,

                    'Authorization: Bearer '.$token,
                    'Content-Type: application/json'
                ),
            ));

            $response = curl_exec($campCurl);
            
            $responseCode = curl_getinfo($campCurl, CURLINFO_HTTP_CODE);
            if ($responseCode != 200) {
                wp_mail("engineering.oncall@hennessey.com,egzon@hennessey.com,alex@hennessey.com", "Camp Lejeune integration data failed on Sinklaw.com", 'ResponseCode: '.$responseCode.' Response: ' . $response . ' Data: ' . var_export($jsonData));
            }

            curl_close($campCurl);
        }
        */
    }
}
